mnemonics: Fred Can't Decide Between Sizes: Flags, Count, Delimiters, Bit Stuffing (to remember the four framing methods: Flag Bytes, Counter-based, Byte Stuffing, Bit Stuffing)
keyPoints: Framing is the process of delineating the start and end of a network frame.
Common methods include flag bytes with byte stuffing, bit stuffing, and using a length field.
The purpose is to allow the receiver to detect and extract the packet from the stream of bits.
commonMistakes: Confusing byte stuffing (used with flag bytes) with bit stuffing (used with bit patterns).
Forgetting that the length field in counter-based framing must account for its own size in the total frame length.
